node.statement: SIGN + CRUDE BOUND. (a) Sign identity: by def-square-hole, h_{r,s}=r o s-P(r o s) and q_{r,s}=P(r o s)-r o s, so h_{r,s}=-q_{r,s} EXACTLY. Hence for any z in B(H)_sa, P(h_{r,s} o z)=-P(q_{r,s} o z), so ||P(h_{r,s} o z)|| = ||P(q_{r,s} o z)|| and any (HZ)/(HH) bound on the q-side transfers verbatim to the h-side. Also h_{r,s} in Ker P: P(h_{r,s})=P(r o s)-P(P(r o s))=P(r o s)-P(r o s)=0 using P^2=P (GT-Pprops). (b) Crude norm bound: ||h_{r,s}|| = ||r o s-P(r o s)|| <= ||r o s||+||P(r o s)|| (triangle inequality for the order-unit norm, GT-bh-cstar). By GT-jb-submult (V=B(H)_sa is a JB algebra via GT-bhsa-jc + GT-jc-is-jb) ||r o s|| <= ||r|| ||s||. By GT-Pprops ||P|| <= 1+C eta, so ||P(r o s)|| <= (1+C eta)||r|| ||s||. Hence ||h_{r,s}|| <= (2+C eta)||r|| ||s|| <= 3||r|| ||s|| (since eta<=eta_0<1/4 and the universal C gives C eta<=1), i.e. ||h_{r,s}|| <= C||r|| ||s|| with the universal constant absorbed.
